Title: Nigel Wong re-elected Matta president for second term
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nigel Wong Chun Teim has been re-elected as president of the Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents (Matta) for the 2026-2029 term, marking his second term leading the association.
The new Executive Council (exco) members elected at the association's 51st Annual General Meeting held at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur on Saturday, comprises seven leaders from the previous term and three new members.
The new leadership team would focus on strengthening talent development, championing industry advocacy, accelerating digital transformation, enhancing member value and expanding international business opportunities over the next three years, Matta said in a statement.
Meanwhile, Wong said he was honoured to be re-elected as Matta president and thanked members for their confidence in the new leadership team.
"One of our greatest achievements over the past term has been strengthening our commitment to serving our members. Every initiative we undertake begins with understanding our members' needs and delivering practical solutions that create value for them," he said.
The new exco, he said, brings together experienced leaders from the previous term and new voices with fresh ideas to support members, champion higher industry standards and position Malaysia as a leading global tourism destination.
The 2026-2029 exco line-up includes Mohd Hizzat Mohd Shah as deputy president and Kho Kwang Peow as honorary treasurer, while the vice-president portfolios cover outbound, air transportation, land transportation, education and training, inbound and domestic, research and technology, as well as umrah and haj.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Venezuela search teams scramble as hope fades of finding quake survivors
Article:
LA GUAIRA, Venezuela: Hopes were fading Monday of finding survivors more than four days after powerful twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as residents grow increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the disaster that has killed at least 1,450 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.
A strong aftershock was felt in the hardest-hit areas of the capital Caracas and La Guaira shortly after 7am (1100 GMT) on Monday, which the US Geological Survey measured at magnitude 4.6, adding to fears for the safety of hundreds of buildings weakened by the tremors.
French and American rescue teams found a man and his teen son alive under the rubble on Sunday in Caraballeda, a town about 40km north of Caracas, AFP journalists saw.
The rescue offered a glimmer of hope in an ongoing tragedy that has shaken a country already mired in an economic crisis, but that hope dwindled as the critical 72-hour window for rescuing trapped victims passed.
Millions more people were feared to lack sanitation and other basic needs after one of Latin America's most devastating earthquake disasters.
Some 774 buildings were badly damaged in back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 that struck on Wednesday evening, including 189 buildings that have totally collapsed, National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez said on Sunday.
AFPTV drone footage from La Guaira showed smoke rising from piles of concrete and debris, where a neighbourhood of multistorey buildings once stood.
In the coastal town of Tucacas, rescuers were digging for people trapped in the pancaked layers and rubble of a collapsed building complex.
Luis Salas, 27, who joined the rescue efforts, told AFP that "the hardest part was when we felt hope in the tunnels we went into – crawling, clearing debris, working with all our heart, with great faith – and when we reached our targets, we found them lifeless."
Experts say the first 72 hours after natural disasters define the narrow window for rescuing the living. After that, the search usually becomes one of recovering bodies.
In the Caracas neighbourhood of Chacao, Caracas, large electronic screens on a building usually used for advertising were showing the faces of missing people.
Last week, UN aid chief Tom Fletcher said that more than 50,000 people were missing.
On Sunday, Rodriguez said the death toll – which was still expected to rise – had reached 1,450 people, with at least 3,150 others injured.
- 'CAN'T DO IT ALONE' -
Even as rescue efforts continued, public anger has mounted in some areas.
Eduardo Cardozo, a volunteer in Tucacas, said it was "frustrating" to know that some victims could have been saved "if they'd been searched for in time."
In La Guaira state's Tanaguarena area, one man urged soldiers to pick up picks and shovels: "The country needs you. Put down your weapon."
Outbreaks of looting have hit La Guaira city, much of which now lies in rubble.
Pharmacies, supermarkets and other businesses were ransacked, said residents, some of whom complained of the slow and meager post-quake aid coming from authorities.
Venezuela's interim president, Delcy Rodriguez, said on Sunday that temporary camps were being set up for people who had lost their homes.
"At the same time, work begins on planning projects that will allow new homes to be built in a very short time," she said.
- ECONOMIC IMPACT -
Rodriguez praised rescuers on Sunday, saying "we have rescued people who are still alive, and therefore these efforts will not be suspended."
"We always hold onto hope."
Cardozo, the Tucacas volunteer, remained hopeful: "We're still here waiting. Let's see if we can get someone else out."
Twenty-four nations have sent 521 tons of supplies, 86 units with dogs trained to locate people trapped beneath the rubble and more than 2,700 search-and-rescue personnel, according to Rodriguez.
US helicopters ferried in aid, and 230 more US military personnel were arriving to help expand airport capacity and reopen a key seaport to boost relief efforts, the US Southern Command said.
The United States – which captured Venezuela's former president Nicolas Maduro in a military raid on Caracas in January – had already sent a 250-strong disaster response team.
The UN migration agency said that based on population and damage data, up to 6.76 million people could be affected, and would require shelter, water, sanitation, healthcare and essential relief items.
Venezuela's worst earthquakes in more than a century have come after the oil-rich country endured more than a decade of economic collapse.
The crisis has hollowed out hospitals and public services, driving millions to leave the country.
The United Nations estimated US$6.7 billion in physical damage – equivalent to six percent of Venezuela's GDP.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump: Iran requested meeting to be held in Doha on Tuesday
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said that Iran has requested a meeting that will be held in the Gulf state of Qatar on Tuesday, despite an earlier denial from Teheran that talks were planned.
"IRAN HAS REQUESTED A MEETING. IT WILL TAKE PLACE TOMORROW IN DOHA!" Trump posted on his Truth Social platform on Monday.
Iran's foreign ministry on Monday denied reports that Iranian and American technical teams will meet this week to discuss the implementation of the deal to end the Middle East war.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rehda Malaysia elects Zaini Yusoff as new president for 2026-2028 term
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Real Estate and Housing Developers' Association (REHDA) Malaysia has elected Datuk Zaini Yusoff as its new president for the 2026-2028 term, succeeding Datuk Ir Ho Hon Sang following the association's annual delegates conference on June 27, 2026.
REHDA said that Ho, who had served as REHDA Malaysia president since June 2024, actively engaged stakeholders from the public and private sectors to address issues affecting the real estate industry during his tenure.
Following his appointment, Zaini, who is from S P Setia Bhd, said that he would do his very best to build upon the strong foundation laid by  Ho and past presidents.
"I would like to thank Datuk Ho for his dedication and diligence over the past two years. I look forward to continue working with you in your new capacity as the immediate past president, together with the other exco and national council members of this esteemed association. With your support, I am committed to carrying out my responsibilities diligently, guided by our principles of being responsive, respected, responsible, and relevant," he said in a statement.
Meanwhile, REHDA Selangor chairperson Datuk Edward Chong Sin Kiat of IJM Corporation Bhd will be taking over Zaini's previous position as the association's deputy president.
Besides Chong's appointment as deputy president, the new leadership line-up also comprises Datuk Charlie Chia Lui Meng and Datuk Ir Tiah Oon Ling as vice-presidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple arrested in RM3.59mil Johor drug bust
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police have dismantled a drug syndicate in Kulai after arresting a married couple and seizing narcotics worth an estimated RM3.59 million.
Detectives from the state police contingent raided a house in the district at 10.50am on June 25.
The suspects, a 40-year-old man and a 34-year-old woman, were arrested after a large quantity of drugs, including MDMA powder, ecstasy pills, Erimin 5 tablets, and ketamine weighing a total of 26.94kg, was uncovered.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the seized drugs could have fed the habits of more than 90,000 users.
"The house was allegedly used as a storage and repackaging centre before the drugs were distributed to the local market," he said.
The couple has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment and mandatory whipping upon conviction.
Ab Rahaman added that the husband, who has prior drug-related offences on his record, also tested positive for methamphetamine.
His wife tested negative for drugs.
Under the Dangerous Drugs (Forfeiture of Property) Act, police also seized RM206,752 worth of assets belonging to the couple, including an undisclosed amount of cash, three vehicles, and jewellery.
Members of the public with information on illicit drug activities can contact the state police narcotics hotline at 012-208 7222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GTA inks partnerships to strengthen aerospace talent pipeline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Global Turbine Asia Sdn Bhd (GTA) has formed partnerships with Universiti Teknikal Malaysia Melaka (UTeM), AMRACE Sdn Bhd and PT3 Consultant Sdn Bhd to bolster talent development and industry collaboration in aerospace sector.
The engine maintenance, repair and overhaul and support services provider said the collaborations are aimed at linking industry players, academic institutions and training providers.
UTeM will support education and academic pathways, while AMRACE and PT3 Consultant will serve as training partners.
GTA said the collaborations underscore its commitment to building a stronger foundation for long-term industry growth and creating opportunities for future talent development.
Executive chairman Datuk Nonee Ashirin Mohd Radzi said the partnerships are to strengthen the aerospace support ecosystem and produce industry-ready talent.
"It is about building a stronger aerospace support ecosystem that can produce future-ready talent, support career transitions and enhance industry capabilities.
"GTA believes sustainable industry growth must be anchored in skilled talent, hands-on training and close collaboration between industry, academia and training partners," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teen assaulted in Tumpat remains traumatised, MP clarifies not rape case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A teenage girl who was robbed and assaulted in an incident in Palekbang, Kelantan, recently remains traumatised, Tumpat MP Datuk Mumtaz Md Nawi told the Dewan Rakyat.
She said the victim was found injured in bushes after the incident on June 18, when she was attacked while jogging with her 10-year-old brother near the area.
The victim, she said, managed to escape during an attempted rape but suffered serious injuries, including facial fractures after being beaten with a stick.
"The teenager was attacked in an attempted rape but managed to escape. The issue is how the media reported that the child was raped when the situation was the opposite," she said when debating the Sexual Offences against Children (Amendment) Bill 2026 today.
Mumtaz urged the government to immediately look into the matter and address concerns over inaccurate reporting of such incidents.
It was reported that
the girl
was later found by a passer-by and taken to hospital for treatment.
The case is being investigated under Section 376 of the Penal Code for rape and Section 394 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt during robbery.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: State Bersatu chief foresees post-election coalition talks
Article:
PAGOH: Johor Bersatu chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal believes no single coalition is likely to secure an outright majority in the upcoming Johor election, raising the prospect of post-election cooperation to form the next state government.
The former menteri besar said Perikatan Nasional (PN) remained confident of performing well despite contesting only 33 of the 56 state seats.
He said PN had electoral understandings with several parties whose political views were broadly aligned with those of the coalition.
"We are confident that in this Johor election, no party will obtain an outright majority.
"Although we are contesting 33 seats, there are parties that share views and positions that are close to ours.
"We have electoral pacts with several parties, so that is not an issue," he told reporters during a walkabout at the Taman Sri Pagoh night market here today.
Dr Sahruddin, who is seeking a third consecutive term as the Bukit Kepong assemblyman, also played down speculation over tensions between Pas and Bersatu following reports that Pas was reviewing its political cooperation with Bersatu.
The former Johor PN chief said leaders and members within the coalition remained focused on strengthening cooperation among its component parties.
"We are under one roof. It is normal for siblings to have disagreements, but the important thing is that they do not leave the house.
"Our responsibility is to strengthen cooperation among Bersatu, Pas, Gerakan and MIPP to ensure victory in future elections. Until today, our opponents remain Pakatan Harapan and Barisan Nasional," he said.
Asked to comment on Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man's call for party members to back BN candidates in seats not contested by PN, Dr Sahruddin declined to elaborate.
However, he reiterated that Bersatu would continue urging voters to support PN candidates.
"In seats contested by PN, voters should support Perikatan Nasional candidates," he said.
Dr Sahruddin first won the Bukit Kepong seat in the 2018 general election and retained it in the 2022 Johor election.
He served as Johor menteri besar from April 2019 to February 2020 and is once again defending the constituency in the state polls.
The Johor election will be held on July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ajinomoto Malaysia names advisers for privatisation bid by Japanese parent
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd has appointed Maybank Investment Bank Bhd as principal adviser for its proposed privatisation by parent company Ajinomoto Co Inc.
RHB Investment Bank Bhd will act as independent adviser for minority shareholders, Ajinomoto Malaysia said in a Bursa Malaysia filing on Monday.
RHB Investment will advise the board, excluding interested directors Daisaku Wadami, Taishi Akiyama and Akihiko Nozaki, as well as disinterested shareholders on the proposed transaction, in line with the rules on take-overs, mergers and compulsory acquisitions.
The appointments follow last week's proposal by Ajinomoto, which owns a 50.38 per cent stake or 30.63 million shares in Ajinomoto Malaysia, to take the company private via a selective capital reduction (SCR) and repayment exercise at RM20 per share.
The offer price represents a 31.6 per cent premium over the stock's last traded price of RM15.20 prior to the announcement on June 22.
Under the proposal, Ajinomoto will pay RM603.4 million to buy and cancel 30.17 million shares held by minority shareholders through the SCR.
As of June 22, the Malaysian unit had an issued share capital of RM65.1 million comprising 60.8 million shares.
To facilitate the exercise, the company plans a bonus issue of 571.1 million shares, which will not be credited to shareholders or listed on Bursa Malaysia.
These shares, together with minority holdings, will be cancelled under the SCR.
The proposal requires approval from at least 75 per cent of votes cast by disinterested shareholders, with not more than 10 per cent voting against, along with other regulatory clearances.
Shares in Ajinomoto Malaysia jumped 25 per cent to RM19 on June 23, the first trading day after the announcement.
The stock closed at RM18.80 on Monday, giving the company a market capitalisation of about RM1.14 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sim racing gains ground as industry expands
Article:
SIM racing is steadily emerging as one of Malaysia's fastest-growing esports segments, supported by increasing government recognition and growing corporate interest.
The esports has gained momentum since being introduced as an official event at the Malaysia Games (SUKMA) in 2024, making Malaysia among the first countries in the world to formally recognise sim racing at a national multi-sport level.
H1GP Sim Racing Championship founder Mika Hakimi said the esports has evolved significantly from being viewed merely as a video game to becoming a legitimate competitive platform with pathways into professional motorsport.
Mika said larger brands and government institutions are increasingly becoming involved in the ecosystem, reflecting the esport's transition into the mainstream.
"Sim racing definitely peaked during the Covid-19 period, and growth has remained healthy since then. The introduction of sim racing into SUKMA shows that the sport is still on the rise," he told Cars, Bikes & Trucks.
Beyond gaming
Hakimi believes sim racing can serve as a stepping stone into professional motorsport, particularly for talented individuals who may not have the financial resources to pursue traditional karting or racing programmes.
"There is a lot of talent out there who simply do not have enough money to get into go-kart racing and sim racing is a great way to spot these talents," he said.
Mika pointed to examples in Europe where automotive manufacturers and motorsport teams have recruited sim racers into professional development programmes, engineering roles and racing teams.
"Porsche for example, have their esports sim racers training in the same building as the engineering department for their motorsport division, so these drivers work together with elite engineers to refine their craft and their knowledge.
"Even non-automotive brands like Red Bull have hired Esports Sim Racers to be a part of their F1 Simulator Program, where the Esports Racer trains with F1 drivers like Max Verstappen," he said.
He believes that Malaysia is well-positioned to become a regional leader in the segment due to strong government support and early adoption.
"Malaysia still has the best adoption so far compared with other regional markets such as Singapore, Thailand and Indonesia," he said.
Building a sustainable ecosystem
Mika said one of the biggest challenges facing the industry remains accessibility, as competition-grade racing simulators can be costly for newcomers.
To address this, a Malaysian sim racing championship and talent development platform, H1GP has been working with schools and communities to bring simulators directly to potential participants through outreach programmes and events.
"The average person would not be able to buy a full simulator in one go. One of the best ways to bridge that gap is to bring the racing simulators to the people," he said.
Founded by Mika, a former national sim racing champion and SUKMA gold medallist, H1GP is positioning itself as more than just a racing league.
The championship has adopted a unique model that rewards drivers financially instead of charging participation fees, a departure from conventional esports and motorsport competitions.
According to Hakimi, the approach is designed to recognise the significant amount of time and effort competitors invest in training and improving their performance.
The championship currently features 25 drivers, with payouts awarded based on performance and achievement targets.
Looking ahead, H1GP aims to become a central platform for the country's sim racing ecosystem while helping to develop future talent, increase participation and attract more commercial investment into the sport.
"I envision that sim racing will be one of the pinnacle disciplines in esports within the next 10 years," Mika added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: PH, PN candidates cross paths at night market during campaign
Article:
PAGOH: Patrons at the Taman Sri Pagoh night market were treated to an unusual sight on the third day of campaigning for the Johor election when two rival candidates crossed paths while greeting voters.
Perikatan Nasional candidate for Bukit Kepong, Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal, and Pakatan Harapan candidate for Bukit Pasir, Muhd Najib Lep, briefly found themselves campaigning in the same area, drawing curious looks from traders and visitors at the bustling night market.
Dr Sahruddin was accompanied by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, while Najib was accompanied by Amanah National Leadership Committee member Mohd Sany Hamzan, who is also the Hulu Langat member of Parliament.
The unexpected encounter took place during separate walkabouts by both candidates, with supporters from both camps accompanying their respective leaders as they mingled with traders and market-goers.
Despite the political rivalry, the situation remained cordial, with both candidates continuing their engagements without incident.
Speaking later to reporters, Dr Sahruddin described  Najib as an old acquaintance and stressed that political competition should not affect personal relationships.
"The Bukit Pasir candidate is actually an old friend. He was previously in Pas, then joined another party, contested as an independent in the last state election, and is now with Amanah.
"We may belong to different political parties, but friendships must be maintained.
"We leave it to the people to decide who the better candidate is.
"However, I believe PN candidates remain steadfast, consistent, honest, and possess integrity," he said.
Dr Sahruddin, a former Johor menteri besar, said he was initially unaware that Najib was also campaigning in the same location.
He said that while it is common for candidates to visit markets during election season, the Taman Sri Pagoh night market falls within the Bukit Kepong constituency.
"If you notice, this area is within Bukit Kepong. The boundary between Bukit Kepong and Bukit Pasir is actually at Sungai Pagoh near the market area.
"The place where we are standing now is in Bukit Kepong, while he is the candidate for Bukit Pasir. So I am not sure why he ended up here," he said jokingly.
When asked whether Najib may have accidentally strayed into his constituency, Dr Sahruddin responded in jest:
"I do not deny your suggestion, but this area is indeed within Bukit Kepong. He did come in a little bit."
Campaigning for the Johor election officially began on Saturday after nominations closed.
Polling is scheduled for July 11, while early voting will take place on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian Media Council seeks PSC review of Cybercrimes Bill
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has called for the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 to be referred to a parliamentary select committee for further scrutiny and improvement, as it infringes on privacy, including using covert operations to obtain private communications.
The council said the bill grants extensive surveillance and intercept power to prevent any crime, under any law, not limited to the cybercrimes it attempts to address.
"The Cybercrimes Bill 2026 lacks the safeguards enclosed in international conventions that the bill is aimed to meet.
"The bill allows law enforcement to infringe on privacy and including using covert operations to obtain private and privileged communications such as between a journalist and source, and between lawyer and client, without any judicial review and authorisation," it said in a statement yesterday.
Without clear statutory safeguards, the exercise of these broad investigative powers could create a chilling effect on investigative journalism, access to justice, whistleblowing, and the ability of citizens to obtain confidential legal advice, it said.
"Given the breadth of powers contemplated by the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 and its potential implications for citizens, businesses, media organisations, legal practitioners, and civil society, there is merit in allowing for more comprehensive public deliberation and expert examination.
"The council respectfully proposes that the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 be referred to the relevant parliamentary select committee for further study, consultation, and debate," it said.
The bill grants extensive powers relating to computer system access, the preservation of computer data, the interception of communications, real-time traffic data collection, search and seizure, and covert investigative activities.
The council raised concerns over Clause 40, which permits the real-time collection of traffic data based solely on the authorisation of the Public Prosecutor, while legally barring service providers from disclosing that this power has been exercised.
It said that powers involving the interception of content data, real-time collection of traffic data, computer system access, and other intrusive measures must be subject to prior judicial approval except in narrowly defined emergency circumstances which should then be immediately followed by judicial review.
The MMC urged the inclusion of a defined retention and review period for data obtained under these powers.
It also recommended a "seriousness threshold" to limit their application to offences outside the cybercrime context, a requirement for periodic reporting on their use, and explicit protection for materials capable of revealing privileged communications or journalistic sources.
"The MMC believes that legislation intended to protect Malaysians from cybercrime must also incorporate robust safeguards to preserve constitutional liberties, maintain public confidence in institutions, and ensure that legitimate journalism and other privileged communications are not inadvertently undermined.
"While the MMC's immediate concern is the protection of journalistic sources and materials, the implications of the bill may extend more broadly to other recognised forms of privileged communication, including exchanges between lawyers and clients protected by legal professional privilege, communications between journalists and whistleblowers, and other confidential relationships deserving of protection under the law," it added.
The Bill, tabled for its first reading last Monday by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, seeks to replace the Computer Crimes Act 1997 with a
wider law covering online crime
, digital fraud, identity theft, manipulated content, and the misuse of digital identification.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RHB Bank fully redeems US$500 mil senior unsecured notes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Bank Bhd has fully redeemed its existing US$500 million senior unsecured notes issued under its Euro Medium Term Note (EMTN) Programme.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the bank said the senior unsecured notes, which were issued on June 29, 2021, have been fully redeemed at their nominal value.
Following the redemption, the notes will be delisted from the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Ltd and Labuan International Financial Exchange Inc.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Advance Profinity unveils oil change scheme with insurance, warranty coverage
Article:
ADVANCE Profinity Sdn Bhd, a distributor of lubricant products, has introduced Automaster Care which combines engine oil change services with insurance and warranty coverage.
Advance Profinity managing director Mohd Harisfathimi Zakaria said the initiative is designed to connect consumers, authorised workshops and the brand within a single ecosystem.
He said the programme is not intended as a short-term campaign, but as a longer-term arrangement to support workshops in remaining competitive amid growing use of aftermarket and DIY parts.
Under the programme, customers who use the company's engine oil at participating workshops will receive four months of personal accident protection.
The coverage includes RM4,000 for accidental death and total permanent disability, up to RM1,500 for bone fractures, RM1,000 for funeral expenses, RM300 for ambulance services and RM150 for medical expenses.
The company is also offering an engine oil warranty programme that provides up to RM10,000 in engine repair coverage.
If an engine failure occurs and investigations confirm it was caused by the engine oil, repair costs will be fully covered by the company.
Registration is conducted digitally in two stages via a dedicated app.
During the first service, customers scan a QR code on the product label, submit vehicle details and upload their receipt.
Full activation requires a second service within five to six months, after which the warranty status is updated to "Fully Active" for an eight-month coverage period.
The programme also allows participating associations such as car clubs, school PTAs, residents' associations, and mosque or surau groups to raise funds.
Members who service their vehicles at authorised workshops can submit proof of purchase through the app, with verified contributions credited to the association's fund account for activities, merchandise or welfare purposes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas tells Johor voters: Don't vote for PH, defend Malay-Muslim power
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas has told voters not to pick Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the upcoming Johor election in its bid to defend Malay-Muslim political power in the state.
In a statement, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said that the party's central leadership held a special meeting today ahead of the July 11 polls.
He said the committee resolved to mobilise the Pas machinery in the 11 contested state seats and to reach out to as many voters as possible from all walks of life under the slogan 'Jom Jaga Johor'.
He added that voters should not cast their ballots for PH, especially in constituencies where PH contests against Barisan Nasional (BN) in straight fights.
The committee is also making early preparations to face the Negri Sembilan election on Aug 1.
Earlier today, Pas Youth said that the party
will back BN candidates
in seats where Perikatan Nasional (PN) is not contesting, in a move to block PH.
Pas Youth information chief Nadzir Helmi said the main objective is to stop the dominance of PH, particularly the influence of DAP as the largest party in the coalition, to safeguard the interests of the people and the future of Johor.
Yesterday, Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri
Takiyuddin Hassan
said the coalition will not side with PH over BN in the 14 seats that will see straight fights between the two blocs.
"There are seats where the contest is only between BN and PH. We have not decided who our members should support in those constituencies.
"It is definitely not PH, but whether we will support BN has yet to be decided," Takiyuddin said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Bersatu only issues directive to vote for PN candidates
Article:
MUAR: Bersatu has not issued any directive allowing its members and supporters to vote for candidates in seats not contested by Perikatan Nasional in the Johor election.
Its president, Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, said this is to avoid confusing party members and supporters over who to vote for in the July 11 polls.
"We take a simple principle. If PN is contesting, we support PN. For other parties contesting against us, why would we support them?
"We should not confuse voters," he told reporters after attending a dialogue session for the Bukit Kepong seat at Kampung Jayor, Pagoh, here today.
Also present was PN candidate from Bersatu for Bukit Kepong, Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal.
"I think almost all seats are being contested. If not by Bersatu, then by other parties or like-minded parties.
"I don't need to explain which parties are aligned with us. That is their decision, their right," Muhyiddin added.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man was quoted as saying that PN supporters should vote for Barisan Nasional candidates in seats not contested by the coalition in the Johor state election in order to prevent a Pakatan Harapan (PH) victory.
He said that in constituencies not contested by PN, the priority was to ensure PH does not win those seats.
However, Tuan Ibrahim stressed that the stance did not mean Pas was forming a political cooperation with BN in the Johor election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australian pipeline assessment technology lands in Malaysia via Insights Analytics
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Insights Analytics Bhd has secured the exclusive rights to distribute and deploy an Australian-developed pipeline condition assessment technology across Malaysia.
The Sarawak-based company signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Australia's Pipeline Inspection & Assessment (PIA) for the rights.
This gave the company exclusive rights to commercialise, deploy and provide after-sales support for PIA's proprietary pipeline condition assessment technology suite called, p-CAT.
Insights Analytics managing director Frank Wee said the partnership marks a significant step in the company's mission to bring world-class pipeline diagnostic technology to Malaysia.
With p-CAT now exclusively under Insights Analytics' distribution, Wee said the company is well positioned to support utilities and regulators in addressing NRW challenges while advancing the nation's water infrastructure agenda.
"We believe this collaboration will create meaningful value for the country's water sector through improved asset visibility, enhanced operational efficiency and more sustainable water management practices," he said.
As an immediate step towards commercialisation, Insights Analytics has begun the country's first p-CAT pilot deployment in collaboration with Lembaga Air Perak, covering about seven kilometres of water pipeline in the state.
p-CAT is a non-destructive, in-pipe diagnostic technology that enables utility operators to assess the structural condition of pipelines without service disruption.
According to Insights Analytics, the technology delivers high-accuracy data on wall thickness degradation, corrosion levels and estimated remaining asset life, supporting more informed asset management and rehabilitation planning.
It noted that the pilot is expected to provide valuable insights into the condition of ageing water infrastructure, enabling utility operators to identify structural vulnerabilities, prioritise rehabilitation works and optimise maintenance expenditure.
Meanwhile, PIA vice president of international partnership for Asia Shazlee Kabeer said deep local expertise and technical capabilities make Insights Analytics the ideal partner to bring p-CAT to the Malaysian market.
"The ongoing pilot deployment with Lembaga Air Perak is an encouraging first step," Shazlee added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 29, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Monday, although the FBM KLCI staged a notable recovery from its intraday lows after heavy selling pressure in the morning session.
Domestically, the standout performer was the banking sector, with financial stocks rebounding strongly and providing the largest support to the FBM KLCI.
The ringgit, meanwhile, strengthened against the US dollar at 4.0700.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin traded lower at RM244,228.
Following the trend, Ethereum traded lower at RM6,417, while Solana gained to RM296.
That's it for Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court orders mental assessment of man who hurt grandmother with hammer
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: The Sessions Court today ordered a 25-year-old man charged with causing grievous hurt to his grandmother with a hammer to undergo a mental health assessment.
Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli issued the order against Muhammad Nor Iman Abdullah following an application by the prosecution, which requested that the accused undergo a psychiatric evaluation before any plea or statement is recorded.
The court ordered him to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak,  to undergo a mental assessment.
Nor Iman is accused of intentionally causing serious injury to his 79-year-old grandmother, Gayah Omar, using a hammer at a house in Kampung Baru Kuala Abang, Dungun, at about 6.30pm on June 19.
He was charged under Section 326 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 326A, which carries a maximum 20 years' jail, a fine and whipping.
Earlier during proceedings, the accused told the court he did not understand the charge after it was read out to him.
The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Nur Aisyah Mohammad, while lawyer Abdul Hayyi Salim from the National Legal Aid Foundation represented the accused.
The court set July 29 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SC issues new guidance note to strengthen Maqasid alignment in sukuk market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commissson (SC) has issued the guidance note on sukuk to reinforce how its issuances align with Maqasid al-Syariah principles.
The initiative forms part of the Capital Market Masterplan 2026-2030, which seeks to promote ethics-driven financial products while supporting the continued development of Malaysia's Islamic capital market.
While current frameworks already ensure Shariah compliance, the new guidance note introduces a more holistic approach to evaluating sukuk.
It encourages Syariah advisers to look beyond compliance and consider broader value creation including alignment with issuers' strategic goals as well as wider economic and social outcomes.
The document sets out the scope and application of the Maqasid al-Syariah guidance within the sukuk ecosystem, and clarifies the respective roles of Syariah advisers and issuers in applying the framework.
It also introduces structured indicators and guiding questions across six key aspirations and 15 underlying principles.
These cover areas such as use of proceeds, sukuk structuring, governance, disclosure and inclusivity.
Practical illustrations are included to show how alignment with Maqasid principles can be assessed in real-world issuances.
The SC has encouraged Syariah advisers and sukuk issuers to integrate these considerations into structuring, fund utilisation and disclosures, while continuing to comply with existing Syariah requirements including resolutions issued by the SC's Syariah Advisory Council and other relevant guidelines.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: BN must build own strength, not rely on others
Article:
TANGKAK: Barisan Nasional must continue to build its own strength instead of relying on the goodwill of other parties to ensure the coalition's victory in the Johor election.
Its deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said every political party must develop its own strength, as only strong parties can earn public confidence and attract support from the people.
Mohamad, fondly known as Tok Mat, however, said the coalition would not reject any party that sincerely wishes to help by voting for BN candidates.
"If there are parties that genuinely want to help and sincerely support BN candidates, we thank them.
"But as I have said before, we must first build our own strength. In politics, we cannot rely too much on the goodwill of others.
"When we are strong, others will come to help us because no one wants to help those who are drowning," Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said when met after delivering a briefing to the BN machinery for the Serom seat at Taman Tanjung Gading here today.
Also present were Ledang Umno division chief Datuk Abdull Rahim Talib and BN candidate for the seat, Nadhirah Afiqah Abdull Rahim.
Mohamad was commenting on Pas' statement suggesting that Perikatan Nasional supporters and voters in constituencies not contested by the coalition should vote for BN candidates in the July 11 polls.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said that while BN appreciates any support given, it does not mean there is any political cooperation between BN and Pas.
He stressed that BN had from the beginning set its position to contest all 56 state seats in the Johor election without any negotiations with any party.
"Claims that there is an understanding or cooperation between the two parties are not true and were never part of our plans.
"We have made it clear from the beginning that BN will contest all 56 seats, so there is no issue of political cooperation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: EC issues over 24,000 postal ballot papers
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Election Commission (EC) has issued 24,677 postal ballot papers for the 16th Johor election.
EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the issuance of postal ballots was conducted today by election officials across all 56 state constituencies.
He said the 24,677 postal ballots comprised 23,288 for Form 1A applicants, which include election officials, EC members and officers; police and armed forces personnel; and media practitioners.
A total of 1,044 postal ballots were issued under Form 1B for Malaysian citizens residing overseas, while 345 were under Form 1C for agencies and organisations.
The issuance process was witnessed by representatives of candidates from all contesting parties and independent candidates.
The EC reminded all postal voters to mark their ballot papers, complete the Declaration of Identity (Form 2) correctly, and return them immediately to their respective state constituency returning officers.
The return envelopes must reach the returning officers before 5pm on polling day this Saturday, July 11.
Voters were also reminded to maintain the secrecy of their votes by not taking photos of their postal ballots or sharing them on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bakat Madani set to benefit 25,000 in first year, says Anwar
Article:
SEREMBAN: Putrajaya's newly-launched Bakat Madani initiative is expected to benefit 25,000 individuals within a year, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
The initiative focuses on enhancing employability and career placement opportunities within government-linked investment companies (GLICs), government-linked companies (GLCs) and Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas).
It also seeks to expand job opportunities across the country's strategic sectors.
Anwar said only a small number of countries are able to offer such opportunities, urging youths to fully understand and make the most of the programme.
He said the success of the initiative will also depend on political stability, unity among the people and a well-organised national system to ensure economic growth benefits society as a whole.
"Given our potential, if the country's system is well-organised, politics remains stable, and the people and society stay united and peaceful, there is much that we can achieve.
"With unity and stability, the government can carefully embrace and harness the economic opportunities available to ensure a better quality of life for the people. Therefore, the first priority is to maintain political stability," he said.
Anwar made the remarks at the launch of the Bakat Madani initiative here today
He said the programme will not only provide opportunities for youths but also create "second chances" for target groups, including B40 households, asnaf groups and vulnerable individuals and armed forces veterans undergoing career transitions.
The government will introduce a special tax deduction for companies that conduct training programmes under the initiative to encourage greater industry participation, with details of the incentive currently being finalised and set to be announced at a later date.
The new incentive builds on the existing employability initiative by expanding coverage to technical and vocational education and training (TVET) graduates and raising the minimum allowance rate to ensure trainees receive fairer and more competitive compensation.
The initiative is a collaborative effort that brings together GLICs, GLCs and Petronas to strengthen national talent development.
It also aims to strengthen TVET institutions through closer industry collaboration to develop a workforce equipped with the skills needed to support the future economy.
Several existing talent development programmes under GLICs, GLCs and Petronas will also be strengthened through the initiative.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Europe's deadly heatwave scorches eastern flank, takes aim at Ukraine
Article:
PARIS: The most severe heatwave ever recorded in Europe roasted central and eastern parts of the continent on Monday and began to creep over Ukraine, which scrambled to prepare its war-ravaged power grid for the shock of scorching heat.
The heatwave first smothered western Europe last week, sending mercury to record highs and straining hospitals, transport networks and power grids on a continent where infrastructure was not built to withstand the punishing temperatures and where air conditioning is not widespread.
More than 1,300 excess deaths were recorded in Europe since June 21, according to the UN health agency, including several small children who died in locked cars and youths who drowned as they sought relief from the infernal temperatures in unsupervised swimming spots.
France reported at least 74 drowning deaths since June 18 and Poland said 17 drowned on Sunday alone.
"I'm doing the same thing as everyone – trying to stay in the shade and drink a lot of water," Susanne, a Vienna resident, told AFP on a bank of a river near the Austrian capital.
"I just hope that the politicians will understand the situation and will begin to set a course in the right direction," she said.
On Monday, the Balkans braced for temperatures of up to 40C, with firefighters in Bosnia battling blazes sparked during the heat.
At least 130 million people in Europe were expected to swelter through temperatures of more than 35C, down from 190 million on Sunday according to an AFP analysis.
This heatwave is the most severe ever recorded in Europe, and would have been "virtually impossible" this early in the summer without climate change, the World Weather Attribution group of scientists said.
All-time temperature records have been broken in Germany, Poland and the Czech Republic, as well as for the month of June in the UK and in Switzerland.
Ukraine on Monday was preparing to absorb the blow from Nature on its energy network, already pummelled by Russian attacks over more than four years of war.
Grid operators in at least five regions – from Ivano-Frankivsk in the west to Zaporizhzhia on the front line in the south – announced temporary restrictions on energy usage would be in force during parts of Tuesday.
The state weather service said the country would face "intense heat", with temperatures of 35C-38C expected on Monday.
"The heat is also a serious test for equipment that has been operating under wartime conditions for more than four years and has withstood numerous attacks," Sergii Kovalenko, CEO of the Yasno energy company said over the weekend.
He said that summer was the peak period for repairing the energy network, battered through the winter by repeat Russian attacks, meaning the grid was already "operating at the limit of its capabilities."
Over the weekend, the heat scorched the Czech Republic, Germany and Poland, with the countries setting new temperature records of 41.9C, 41.7C and 40.5C, respectively.
The Berlin police used water cannons to help residents of the capital cool off for a second day running Sunday – this time at the Olympia venue where singer Bruno Mars was performing.
With temperatures cooling in France, the national weather service said on Sunday evening it was already anticipating the possibility of another heatwave in July.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas Youth: Vote shift to BN in some seats aimed at blocking PH
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pas Youth has clarified that the party's readiness to back Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in seats where Perikatan Nasional (PN) is not contesting is a strategic move to block Pakatan Harapan (PH).
Pas Youth information chief Nadzir Helmi said the statement by the Pas deputy president must be understood within a broader political strategy.
He said the main objective is to halt the dominance of PH, particularly the influence of DAP as the largest party in the coalition, to safeguard the interests of the people and the future of Johor.
"Pas Youth believes that breaking PH's dominance is a necessity that all parties must understand. In today's political climate, every vote carries strategic value in determining the direction of the state administration after the election," he said.
He said the primary duty of every Pas member is to vote and ensure victory for all PN candidates.
"Votes from Pas members must be given to all candidates contesting under the PN ticket, regardless of their component party.
"Meanwhile, any consideration to vote for candidates outside PN in certain seats should be viewed strictly as a tactical strategy to prevent PH from advancing," he said.
He said the strategy is not a general policy and could change based on current needs and developments.
Pas' priorities remain consistent, which are to strengthen Malay-Muslim political dominance, secure as many PN victories as possible and block PH and DAP from controlling Johor.
This stance forms the foundation of all Pas political decisions, said Nadzir.
He urged all members and supporters not to be influenced by narratives attempting to confuse the public.
"Maintain struggle discipline, obey party instructions and prioritise a PN victory as the main agenda in the Johor state election. Votes must go to PN. Political strategies in other seats are meant to check PH's advance. Our priority remains the same, which is defending the interests of Islam, the Malays and the future of Johor," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Parliament sends condolences to senator Awang Bemee over mother's death
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul today conveyed Parliament's condolences to Dewan Negara president Senator Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah following the death of his mother.
Johari spoke in the Dewan Rakyat after Kuala Langat member of parliament Datuk Dr Ahmad Yunus Hairi concluded his debate on the second reading of the Sexual Offences against Children (Amendment) Bill.
"Before I invite Tampin, I would like to make an announcement.
"It is with deep sadness that I inform the House that the mother of Dewan Negara President Senator Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah, Sehat Rasdi, died a short while ago," he said.
"I hope Senator Datuk Awang Bemee Awang Ali Basah and the entire family remain strong during this difficult time.
"We pray that Allah will shower His mercy upon her soul and place her among the righteous and the faithful," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Plus Xnergy launches one of Malaysia's largest self-consumption BESS
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Plus Xnergy Services Sdn Bhd (Plus Xnergy) has commissioned one of Malaysia's largest single-deployment self-consumption (battery energy storage systems (BESS) to optimise electricity consumption.
Plus Xnergy group chief executive officer Ko Chuan Zhen said the project delivery marks a significant milestone in Malaysia's clean energy transition.
"Based on our data, the savings is projected to be around RM1.25 million per annum for every 10 MWh of BESS depending on load profile under the Regulatory Period 4 framework.
"This project serves as a live business case for energy-intensive sectors, such as large-scale food processing and heavy manufacturing, seeking to enhance their competitiveness while fulfilling corporate sustainability goals," Ko said in a statement.
Developed in collaboration with global energy storage solutions provider HyperStrong, Plus Xnergy said the 20 megawatt-hour BESS has reached full commercial operation at a major food manufacturing facility in Perak.
By charging during off-peak periods and discharging during peak demand hours, it said the system optimises energy utilisation, smooths load fluctuations, improves localised power supply stability and significantly reduces maximum demand charges for the manufacturer.
"The resulting set-up offers a highly cost-effective and reliable mitigator against rising utility expenses while supporting the facility's long-term operational and decarbonisation objectives," it said.
Plus Xnergy is offering deployment of these set-ups through a zero-capital expenditure Performance Sharing Agreement to accelerate market adoption amid high operating costs to all industrial manufacturers.
Under the 15-year commercial partnership model, Plus Xnergy fully finances, engineers, and manages the asset on-site, allowing industrial players to capture immediate energy tariff reductions with zero initial capital outlay.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza health officials say 3 killed in Israeli drone strike
Article:
GAZA CITY: Gaza health officials said an Israeli drone strike killed three civilians and injured several others on Monday, the latest violence to hit the Palestinian territory despite a ceasefire.
Israel and Hamas trade near-daily accusations of truce violations and the Gaza Strip remains gripped by bloodshed as progress stalls on permanently ending their war.
"Three people were killed and several injured when an Israeli drone struck a group of civilians," Al-Aqsa hospital said in a statement.
Gaza's civil defence agency, which acts as a rescue force under Hamas, said the strike hit an area in Deir el-Balah in central Gaza.
An Israeli military source confirmed the attack.
"The results of the strike are still being assessed," the source told AFP.
At least 1,041 Palestinians have been killed in Gaza since the ceasefire took effect on Oct 10 last year, according to the territory's health ministry, which operates under Hamas authority and whose figures are considered reliable by the United Nations.
The Israeli army has reported six deaths in its ranks during the same period.
Restrictions imposed on media outlets and limited access in Gaza prevent AFP from independently verifying tolls or freely covering the violence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Strengthening resilience and energy security in Malaysia
Article:
AS United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres underscored during the recent London Climate Action Week, the climate and energy crises must be addressed together and at their root.
Continued reliance on fossil fuels exposes economies to volatile energy prices, geopolitical shocks and growing climate risks, while slowing progress towards sustainable development.
The answer lies in a fast, fair and equitable transition to clean energy. In Malaysia, that transition is already taking shape through a growing set of policies and initiatives that link climate ambition with energy security, economic competitiveness and resilience.
A key signal of this momentum is Malaysia's Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) 3.0, submitted in 2025 ahead of COP30.
The updated commitment strengthens national climate ambition by moving beyond an emissions-intensity approach towards an absolute emissions reduction target, with emissions projected to peak between 2029 and 2034 before declining by 2035 across all major sectors.
This represents a significant shift and sends a clear signal that economic growth and rising emissions can no longer remain coupled.
Delivering on climate commitments requires a practical pathway that aligns environmental objectives with economic priorities.
The National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR)  therefore provides strategic direction for Malaysia's broader economic and energy transformation, linking decarbonisation with competitiveness, investment and long-term energy security.
As energy demand continues to rise, driven by industrial development, digitalisation and emerging sectors such as artificial intelligence, Malaysia must ensure that future growth is supported by a more resilient and sustainable energy system.
In that context, the environmental footprint of data centres and AI infrastructure is emerging as an important policy consideration globally and in Malaysia.
Achieving this vision requires accelerating renewable energy deployment, improving energy efficiency and strengthening grid flexibility.
As highlighted by the Secretary-General, renewables are now the cheapest and fastest-growing source of new electricity in most parts of the world.
A cleaner and more diversified energy system can reduce exposure to global fuel price volatility, enhance energy security, attract sustainable investment and create new opportunities in green industries.
Building resilience must therefore advance alongside decarbonisation. Malaysia is strengthening its climate resilience architecture through the development of its first National Adaptation Plan (MyNAP).
By integrating climate risk into development planning, Malaysia is helping to build resilience for current and future generations.
The preparation of a national Climate Change Bill is an important step towards embedding climate action within Malaysia's legal and institutional framework.
A clear legislative foundation can strengthen accountability, clarify responsibilities and reinforce monitoring, reporting and verification systems.
As a signatory to the Global Methane Pledge, Malaysia is part of a global effort to reduce methane emissions by at least 30 per cent by 2030 compared with 2020 levels.
Ultimately, the success of this transition will depend on people as much as technologies and regulations.
Youth engagement is increasingly shaping Malaysia's climate agenda, including through joint initiatives supported by Unicef and UNDP.
Young people are not only future beneficiaries of climate action, they are active contributors to solutions today.
[[nid:1476026]]
Their participation brings intergenerational equity into climate decision-making and helps ensure that the transition remains inclusive and socially grounded.
Supporting this transformation requires collaboration across governments, businesses, communities and international partners.
Malaysia's climate transition is being supported by a wide range of national stakeholders, with the UN contributing expertise, partnerships and global experience.
Across the UN family, agencies are supporting different dimensions of this transformation.
UNDP is working with partners to unlock climate finance and advance a just transition via the "Climate Promise", a UN System-wide effort, while UNIDO (United Nations Industrial Development Organisation) supports industrial decarbonisation and the deployment of innovative solutions, including smart grid technologies that enable greater integration of renewable energy and enhance grid flexibility.
More broadly, the United Nations provides an important platform for coordination among stakeholders, helping to build trust, facilitate dialogue and promote shared standards in support of climate action and implementation of the Paris Agreement.
Energy security, economic competitiveness, climate resilience and social inclusion are not competing priorities; they are interconnected outcomes of the same transformation.
Taken together, NDC 3.0, the National Energy Transition Roadmap, MyNAP, the forthcoming Climate Change Bill, Malaysia's participation in the Global Methane Pledge and its engagement under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement demonstrate a coherent and increasingly ambitious national climate agenda.
They reflect a country that is steadily integrating climate action into its development pathway while recognising the opportunities that a low-carbon and climate-resilient future can bring.
The climate and energy crises share a common origin, but they also share a common solution.
By advancing a clean energy transition that is inclusive, credible and well-governed, Malaysia is well-positioned to strengthen energy security, reduce long-term risks and accelerate sustainable development while remaining aligned with its aspiration of achieving net-zero emissions by 2050.
Under the leadership of the UN Resident Coordinator, the United Nations Country Team stands ready to support Malaysia in this transition, working in partnership with the government and stakeholders to deliver integrated, coordinated, and impactful solutions.
As we approach the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development milestone, one message is clear: the transition is not only necessary, it must accelerate.
* The writer is Coordinator for Climate, Biodiversity and Pollution, Office of the UN Resident Coordinator for Malaysia, Singapore and Brunei
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN manifesto realistic, youths the biggest winners, says leader
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Barisan Nasional (BN) manifesto for the 16th Johor election is a realistic blueprint that champions youth by focusing on their actual needs, says Johor BN Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros.
He said the manifesto, launched by Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, avoids empty promises and offers actionable commitments.
"This manifesto does not promise the moon and stars. That is what is most important. We are not like the opposing side. What we offer is realistic and directly answers the demands and hopes of youth in Johor," he said in a video shared on Facebook today.
Noor Azleen, who is also Johor Umno Youth chief, said the manifesto focuses on ensuring the younger generation receives better educational opportunities, quality employment and assistance in owning their first home.
He said education remains a priority as the foundation for the future of young people, highlighted by strengthened aid for university students, laptop assistance and efforts to open pathways for youth participation in municipal councils.
He said BN understands the hurdles graduates face when entering the job market.
"Young people do not just want jobs; they want quality jobs that offer premium, dignified wages. Therefore, the target to create
200,000 new job opportunities
with a starting salary of at least RM4,000 is realistic, aligning with efforts to transform Johor into a developed state by 2030," he said.
He said BN also addresses homeownership through various support mechanisms to help young people secure their own homes.
Assistance for first-home deposits, move-in grants and rental aid shows that the state government understands their daily struggles, he said.
He said when education, employment and housing are addressed comprehensively, it proves that BN is genuinely pro-youth and committed to helping them build a better future.
Earlier, Onn Hafiz launched the Johor BN manifesto, themed "Maju Johor, Kestabilan Dikekalkan, Kemajuan Diteruskan" (Progress Johor, Stability Maintained, Development Continued), outlining 63 pledges across six pillars.
Key offers for youth include expanding the RM100 million Johor Youth Education Fund, continuing undergraduate incentives such as laptop aid and expanding youth representation in local government.
The manifesto also guarantees the creation of 200,000 quality jobs, a first-home deposit assistance scheme, entry grants and rental subsidies to help more Johor youth secure stable lives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Consulate: Malaysians told to be on alert amid heavy rain warning in southern Thailand
Article:
SONGKHLA, June 29 — The Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla is urging Malaysians in southern Thailand and those travelling to the province to be on alert, following a heavy to very heavy rain forecast in several areas of the country from June 28 to July 3.
In an advisory on Monday, the consulate said the warning was issued by the Department of Disaster Prevention and Mitigation (DDPM) of Thailand.
According to the consulate, the weather condition could trigger flash floods, widespread flooding, forest runoffs, landslides, and rough seas along the Andaman Sea coastline.
“Malaysians in Thailand and those looking to visit the country, especially areas that have been identified as at-risk, are therefore advised to remain vigilant, monitor the latest weather updates, and adhere to instructions and advices issued by Thai authorities from time to time,” it said.
The Malaysian Consulate General also encourages Malaysian citizens to obtain adequate travel insurance before their departures to minimise the risks of losses or disruptions that might arise from unforeseen circumstances while in Thailand.
The consulate also advises the public to refrain from visiting waterfalls, caves, hillsides, rivers, and beaches that have been identified as at-risk, especially in the event the weather deteriorates or local authorities restrict access to the areas.
Malaysian citizens requiring consular assistance may contact the duty officer at the Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla by calling +66 81-990-1930 or e-mailing mwsongkhla@kln.gov.my.
The Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla also informed that it would continue to monitor the weather and situational developments in Thailand and convey the latest information as required.
The warning comes as several provinces in southern Thailand are still bracing for unpredictable weather following the monsoon season.
At the end of November and December last year, the Songkhla province, including the city of Hat Yai, was hit by a major flood as a result of prolonged downpours. The flood inundated residential areas and commercial centres, disrupted transportation networks, and caused thousands of residents to be displaced.
The event also affected travels by Malaysians who entered southern Thailand via the land border crossing. — Bernama

Title: PM: 25,000 Malaysians will benefit from new Bakat Madani initiative
Article:
SEREMBAN, June 29 — A total of 25,000 people are set to benefit from the Bakat Madani initiative launched today, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the initiative is a national effort involving government-linked investment companies (GLICs), government-linked companies (GLCs), and Petronas to boost national talent development, focusing on skills development, the creation of quality jobs and improving social mobility.
“The programme provides exposure to about 25,000 individuals. Young people should not forget to thank the corporate bodies that have helped ensure its proper implementation.
“If they do not manage it properly, the programme cannot be carried out as the financing is provided by them, not by me,” he said when officiating the programme, which was also attended by Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
Also present were Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, ministers, secretaries-general of ministries, as well as leaders of GLICs, GLCs and Petronas.
Meanwhile, Amir Hamzah said the initiative emphasises three main areas: strengthening employability and career placement within the GLIC, GLC and Petronas ecosystem; expanding job placement opportunities across key strategic sectors; and empowering Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions.
“Our task is to connect talent with the opportunities that exist, and that is the primary goal of Bakat Madani.
“Investing in human development also strengthens the nation’s economic base,” he said, adding that improving living standards and economic growth reinforce each other in a virtuous cycle.
He said priority is given to high-value sectors with strong growth potential, including semiconductors, renewable energy, the digital economy and advanced manufacturing.
Amir Hamzah said the government will introduce special tax incentives to firms running training programmes under Bakat Madani, aimed at boosting industry involvement in skills development and quality employment.
“The new incentive is an improvement on existing employability measures, with expanded coverage to TVET graduates and a higher minimum allowance to ensure trainees receive fair and competitive compensation,” he said.
Several talent development programmes are being enhanced under the Bakat Madani initiative, including the transformation of VISTA into Vista i-Plus by Petronas with Malaysian Petroleum Resources Corporation (MPRC) and the Malaysian Oil, Gas & Energy Services Council (MOGSC), creating an integrated TVET training model involving institutions such as MARA Skills Institutes (IKM), National Youth Skills Institutes (IKBN), Advanced Technology Training Centres (ADTEC) and the Malaysian Construction Academy (ABM).
Under the GLIC and GLC ecosystem, Khazanah Nasional Berhad is working with 23 higher education institutions, including Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM), Universiti Teknikal Malaysia Melaka (UTeM) and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), to prepare young talent for the workforce through industrial training, technical certification and exposure to industry requirements. — Bernama

Title: PM: Malaysia, Indonesia committed to strengthening cooperation in legislation, governance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia and Indonesia are committed to strengthening cooperation in the fields of legislation, governance and various strategic areas of mutual interest, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In a Facebook post, Anwar said he discussed the matter with Indonesia’s Coordinating Minister for Legal, Human Rights, Immigration and Correction, Prof Dr Yusril Ihza Mahendra, who paid him a courtesy call at the Prime Minister’s Office in Putrajaya today.
During the meeting, Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said both leaders also exchanged views on efforts to strengthen cooperation in those fields.
“May the close fraternal relations and cooperation between the two countries continue to be strengthened for the progress, peace, and well-being of the people of Malaysia and Indonesia,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Couple hospitalised after wild mushroom meal in Padang Besar
Article:
SIK, June 29 — A husband and wife from Padang Besar were rushed to hospital after suffering severe food‑poisoning symptoms believed to be caused by eating wild mushrooms picked near a cattle shed at their home.
Berita Harian
reported that the couple, Muhd Basir Abidudin Abd Razak and Intan Nabila Mazelam, both 28, reportedly cooked the mushrooms for lunch before collapsing hours later. According to the Briged Sukarela Yayasan Jariah Malaysia (BSYJM), rescuers received an emergency call at about 4pm and found the pair in a bathroom, weak, vomiting and suffering from diarrhoea.
BSYJM chairman Mahadzir Abd Hamid said the situation was dire, with the house “covered in vomit and faeces” and both victims unable to stand. The rescue was challenging as each victim weighed more than 150kg, requiring six BSYJM members and villagers to lift them into two ambulances. They were taken to Hospital Daerah Sik for treatment.
Mahadzir said Intan Nabila had contacted her mother and brother‑in‑law earlier to check whether the mushrooms were safe to eat, but the family mistakenly believed they were edible. The mushrooms were later determined to be poisonous.
The couple’s condition has not yet been publicly disclosed, and authorities are reminding the public to avoid consuming wild mushrooms unless properly identified by experts.

Title: After viral Sabah hospital post, Sabah Health Dept cites familiar strain and overcrowding
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 29 — Sabah's Health Department has responded to a viral Facebook post depicting the crowded conditions at Queen Elizabeth Hospital (QEH), highlighting the overstretched public hospital’s infrastructure and manpower.
The statement came after a Facebook user named Sualim Gopog shared his experience accompanying a family member to the hospital's Emergency and Trauma Department (ETD) in the early hours of the morning including slow moving queues and many people found sleeping in the lobby and outside the hospital building.
In the widely shared
post
, Gopog described seeing dozens of people sleeping on benches and on the floor of the corridors, relatives searching for food after the hospital canteen closed at 12am, near-impossible parking, and patients waiting hours to be seen.
He noted that although his family member's queue number moved slowly — from 149 to 143 after nearly two hours — he did not witness any complaints from those waiting.
The Sabah Health Department said it ran a preliminary investigation and downplayed the situation, citing the realities of the state’s busiest hospital serving as the main referral center.
“Patients at the ETD are treated according to the severity of their condition under the triage system, and not solely based on queue numbers. More critical cases are prioritised for immediate treatment.
“On the night in question, ETD services operated as usual with a continuous influx of patients. In the Green Zone, a total of 42 patients were recorded between 9pm and 10am, with two medical officers on duty to attend to non-critical cases,” they said in a statement here today.
Regarding images showing members of the public resting or sleeping in the hospital lobby and corridors at night, the department said that they were not patients waiting for treatment or appointments at the ETD.
“Most of them were family members, caregivers or visitors from outside Kota Kinabalu and Sabah's interior who were accompanying patients and faced difficulties securing temporary accommodation outside the hospital.
“QEH has a visitors' hostel; however, it is insufficient to accommodate the large number of patients' relatives,” they said.
They also clarified that the hospital’s canteen usually operates 24 hours a day, except for a temporary closure every Saturday from midnight until 5am to allow for scheduled cleaning works.
The department also acknowledged the limited parking at QEH, a perennial problem plaguing the hospital for years.
“The hospital will continue to monitor traffic flow and parking management in collaboration with its security personnel to reduce congestion as far as existing facilities allow.
“As part of ongoing improvements, QEH will strengthen monitoring of the lobby, public corridors and waiting areas at night, review communication regarding canteen operating hours, and improve communication with patients and their families on waiting procedures and treatment priorities at the ETD,” they said.
In the viral post, Sualim praised the dedication of the doctors who worked tirelessly throughout the shift.
Despite a decent record of services provided, Sabahans have for years been complaining about the state’s inadequate healthcare facilities including severe shortage of doctors, particularly at major referral centres such as Queen Elizabeth Hospital, which serves patients from across Sabah.
The federal government has promised temporary upgrades as plans are put into place to provide better services but the effects have yet to be felt on the ground.

Title: Bomb blast in Narathiwat: Wisma Putra confirms no Malaysian casualties
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 29 — Malaysia’s Foreign Ministry is monitoring developments in southern Thailand after an improvised explosive device (IED) detonated in Narathiwat province today, with a Malaysian‑registered vehicle reportedly caught in the blast.
The explosion occurred at about 11.45am local time, according to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA). In a preliminary update, Wisma Putra said no Malaysian fatalities have been reported, and the Consulate General in Songkhla is in contact with Thai authorities to verify details and extend consular assistance where needed.
Malaysians living in or travelling through Thailand’s southern provinces were urged to remain alert and comply strictly with local security directives. The ministry also encouraged citizens to register their contact details on the e‑Konsular portal to ensure timely communication during emergencies.
For urgent assistance, Malaysians may contact the Consulate General of Malaysia in Songkhla, which can be reached via several telephone and WhatsApp lines, as well as email.
Wisma Putra said it will continue monitoring the situation and issue further updates once verified information becomes available.

Title: Can-meh? Yes, new MEH licence plates for Melaka will open for bidding from July 10
Article:
MELAKA, June 29 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) Melaka has issued a public notice encouraging Malaysians to participate in its latest round of vehicle registration number bidding via the JPJ eBid online platform for plates starting with ‘MEH’
In a Facebook post today, JPJ Melaka urged motorists and vehicle owners to register and submit bids through jpjebid.jpj.gov.my, the department’s official digital bidding system.
Bids for MEH plates will start July 10 and close on July 14, with bid results announced on July 15.
The department also used several campaign hashtags — including #DigitalisasiMemacuProfesionalismeJPJ, #JPJMalaysia, and #PanduCermatSampaiSelamat — underscoring its broader digitalisation drive and road‑safety messaging.
JPJ eBid, introduced nationwide in recent years, allows users to bid for preferred number plates without visiting JPJ counters. The system has become a popular avenue for securing special or highly sought‑after series, with bids conducted entirely online and results released digitally.
JPJ Melaka did not specify which number series is currently open for bidding, but similar announcements typically precede the release of new state or national plate sequences.
Members of the public can register for an account on JPJ eBid and submit bids before the closing date indicated on the platform.

Title: High Court rejects former directors’ bid to delay RM492,480 refund owed to umrah pilgrims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The High Court here today dismissed an application by three former directors and shareholders of a tourism company seeking to postpone repayment of RM492,480 to umrah pilgrims pending the outcome of their appeal.
Judge Leong Wai Hong dismissed the application by Datuk Dr Fathul Bari Mat Jahya, Sekh Mohd Fazzli Sekh Mohd Ruzi and Wan Azizul Wan Yusoff to stay execution of the payment order.
“The grounds of appeal filed do not constitute special circumstances that justify granting a stay. Accordingly, the application is dismissed with costs of RM5,000,” the judge ruled.
